title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of Q1'26
content:
The credit union's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though overall loan balances declined -4.6% from the previous period. Total loans of $58,711 thousand remain concentrated in Used Vehicle and First Lien Real Estate, which continue to represent the primary sources of both earnings and credit risk.

Portfolio Composition
The credit union's loan portfolio continues to be primarily concentrated in Used Vehicle. As of Q1'26, Used Vehicle totaled 29,611 (50.4%) and New Vehicle totaled 2,504 (4.3%), together representing approximately 54.7% of the total portfolio.

Portfolio Composition
All Other Secured Non-Real Estate Loans/LOCs totaled 1,976 (3.4%), while All Other Unsecured loans accounted for 3,239 (5.5%). Overall loan balances declined -4.6% from the previous period, reflecting continued balance-sheet contraction.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Credit performance remains manageable despite modest delinquency levels within certain segments. Total delinquent loans fell to a figure not explicitly quantified in thousands but represented by the stacked bar chart for Q1'26 showing a reduction from prior periods. The largest delinquency exposure remains in Used Vehicle (50,000) and First Lien Real Estate (significant portion), followed by All Other Secured Non-Real Estate.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
The concentration of delinquencies within Used Vehicle and First Lien Real Estate remains consistent with the overall portfolio composition. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
